enrichment.short_description: Gem is an AI-first recruiting platform that unifies sourcing, CRM, ATS, scheduling, and analytics for talent acquisition teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Gem is an AI recruiting platform combining sourcing, CRM, ATS, and analytics — used by 1,200+ talent acquisition teams including GitLab and Figma.

features.title: AI Sourcing Agent
features.description: Searches 800M+ profiles and surfaces past candidates with verified contact info.

features.title: AI Application Review Agent
features.description: Ranks applicants using AI reasoning to speed up application review.

features.title: AI Fraud Detection Agent
features.description: Flags fraudulent or AI-generated candidate applications.

features.title: CRM
features.description: Manages candidate pipelines with automated nurture sequences.

features.title: ATS
features.description: Handles job postings, pipeline stages, and offer approvals.

features.title: Scheduling
features.description: Automates interview booking.

features.title: Analytics
features.description: Provides hiring forecasts and pipeline insights.

features.title: ATS Integrations
features.description: Connects with Greenhouse, Workday, Lever, and iCIMS.

pros: AI sourcing agent searches over 800 million candidate profiles
Combines ATS, CRM, sourcing, and analytics in one platform
Integrates with major ATS providers (Greenhouse, Workday, Lever, iCIMS)
Used by 1,200+ talent acquisition teams including Anthropic, GitLab, and Figma
Startup program offers 6 months free plus a discount afterward
cons: Full platform pricing is quote-based and tied to company FTE count
No dedicated iOS or Android apps mentioned on the site
AI-powered features are gated to a separate seat type from standard seats

faqs.question: What does Gem do?
faqs.answer: Gem is an AI-first recruiting platform that combines sourcing, CRM, ATS, scheduling, and analytics into one system for talent acquisition teams.

faqs.question: Does Gem replace my existing ATS?
faqs.answer: Gem offers two options: "Gem + Your ATS," which layers AI sourcing on top of an existing ATS, or "Gem All-in-One," which includes its own built-in ATS.

faqs.question: How much does Gem cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is custom based on company size; Gem's startup program lists a discounted rate of $130/month (billed annually) for companies with 1-10 employees.

faqs.question: Does Gem offer a free plan for startups?
faqs.answer: Companies under 30 employees can get free access for six months, then 50% off paid plans.

faqs.question: What AI features does Gem include?
faqs.answer: Gem offers an AI Sourcing Agent, an AI Application Review Agent, and an AI Fraud Detection Agent.

use_cases.title: Sourcing passive candidates at scale
use_cases.description: Recruiters use Gem's AI Sourcing Agent to search 800M+ profiles and resurface past candidates with verified contact info.

use_cases.title: Replacing a fragmented recruiting stack
use_cases.description: Companies use Gem All-in-One to combine ATS, CRM, sourcing, and analytics instead of running separate tools.

use_cases.title: Screening high application volumes
use_cases.description: Teams use the AI Application Review Agent to rank and flag fraudulent applicants faster during high-volume hiring.

features: AI sourcing layered on existing ATS

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Gem?
paragraphs: Gem is an AI-first recruiting platform that brings sourcing, CRM, ATS, scheduling, and analytics together in a single system.
It layers AI agents for sourcing, application review, and fraud detection on top of a candidate pipeline, and can either connect to an existing ATS or serve as a company's full ATS.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Gem is for
paragraphs: Gem is built for talent acquisition teams at startups, growing companies, enterprises, and recruiting firms that need to source, engage, and track candidates in one place. Gem runs a discounted startup program for companies under 100 employees.

enrichment.short_description: Fetcher is an AI-powered candidate sourcing platform that delivers qualified, engaged candidates to recruiting teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Fetcher is an AI candidate sourcing platform with tiered plans from $115/month, giving recruiters access to a 500M+ talent database.

features.title: AI-Screened Sourcing
features.description: Surfaces and screens candidates using AI paired with a sourcing team.

features.title: Candidate Engagement
features.description: Tools to reach out to and engage sourced candidates.

features.title: Recruitment Analytics
features.description: Reporting on sourcing performance.

features.title: Diversity Search Criteria
features.description: Lets recruiters customize searches around diversity goals.

features.title: ATS/CRM Integrations
features.description: Connects with existing ATS, CRM, email, and Slack tools.

features.title: Candidate Database Access
features.description: All plans include access to a 500M+ candidate database.

pros: Transparent published pricing tiers starting at $115/month
Access to a 500M+ candidate database on every plan
Higher tiers include a dedicated sourcer for open roles
Integrates with existing ATS, CRM, email, and Slack
cons: No free trial or free plan listed
Entry-level Self-Serve plan is limited to one user seat and 300 leads/month
Enterprise-level pricing requires a custom quote

faqs.question: How much does Fetcher cost?
faqs.answer: Fetcher has three published plans: Self-Serve at $115/month, Growth at $379/month, and Amplify at $649/month, plus a custom Enterprise tier, with 30% off for annual billing.

faqs.question: Does Fetcher offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No free trial or free plan is listed on Fetcher's pricing page.

faqs.question: What's included in every Fetcher plan?
faqs.answer: All plans include access to Fetcher's 500M+ candidate database.

faqs.question: Does Fetcher provide a dedicated sourcer?
faqs.answer: The Amplify plan includes a dedicated sourcer for 4-6 roles and a dedicated success team.

faqs.question: Where is Fetcher based?
faqs.answer: Fetcher's office is listed at 287 Park Avenue South, New York, NY.

use_cases.title: Sourcing candidates without an internal team
use_cases.description: Recruiters short on sourcing bandwidth use Fetcher's Self-Serve or Growth plans to get AI-screened candidates.

use_cases.title: Scaling high-volume hiring
use_cases.description: Companies with multiple open roles use the Amplify plan's dedicated sourcer and success team.

features: For large companies and agencies with high-volume needs

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Fetcher?
paragraphs: Fetcher is an AI-powered candidate sourcing platform that pairs automated screening with a sourcing team to deliver qualified candidates to recruiters.
It offers tiered plans that scale from self-serve sourcing to a fully dedicated sourcing team, all built on a 500M+ candidate database.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Fetcher is for
paragraphs: Fetcher serves recruiting teams hiring across engineering, finance, product, ops, marketing, sales, and customer success roles, from individual recruiters to larger teams needing dedicated sourcing support.

enrichment.short_description: AmazingHiring is a talent sourcing platform that searches 50+ networks like GitHub and Stack Overflow to find tech candidates.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: AmazingHiring helps recruiters source tech talent by searching 50+ developer networks including GitHub, Stack Overflow, and LinkedIn.

features.title: Multi-Network Search
features.description: Searches GitHub, Stack Overflow, Kaggle, LinkedIn, and other developer communities.

features.title: Direct Contact Discovery
features.description: Surfaces direct contact information for candidates.

features.title: Email Campaigns
features.description: Send outreach campaigns to sourced candidates.

features.title: Chrome Extension
features.description: Free browser extension that surfaces a candidate's social and professional links.

features.title: Market Insights
features.description: Talent market data to inform sourcing decisions.

features.title: ATS Integrations
features.description: Connects with applicant tracking systems.

pros: Searches over 50 technical and developer networks in one place
Free Chrome extension for surfacing candidate profile links
Used by 500+ talent acquisition teams
Surfaces direct contact info, not just profile links
cons: Pricing is not published on the website
No explicit AI branding despite advanced search capabilities
No dedicated mobile app beyond the browser extension

faqs.question: What does AmazingHiring do?
faqs.answer: AmazingHiring is a sourcing platform that searches over 50 networks such as GitHub, Stack Overflow, Kaggle, and LinkedIn to find technical candidates.

faqs.question: Is AmazingHiring free?
faqs.answer: The Chrome extension is free; full platform pricing is not published and requires contacting sales.

faqs.question: Does AmazingHiring integrate with an ATS?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers ATS integrations, though specific systems aren't named on the site.

faqs.question: Who uses AmazingHiring?
faqs.answer: The site states it is trusted by 500+ talent acquisition teams, including Talentful, Lilium, Bolt, and Nortal.

use_cases.title: Sourcing hard-to-find tech candidates
use_cases.description: IT recruiters use AmazingHiring's multi-network search to find developers not active on LinkedIn.

use_cases.title: Quick candidate lookup while browsing
use_cases.description: Recruiters use the free Chrome extension to surface a candidate's social and professional links while viewing a profile.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is AmazingHiring?
paragraphs: AmazingHiring is a talent sourcing platform focused on technical hiring. It searches more than 50 networks, including GitHub, Stack Overflow, Kaggle, and LinkedIn, to help recruiters find candidates and their contact information.
It also offers a free Chrome extension, email outreach campaigns, and market insights for sourcing decisions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who AmazingHiring is for
paragraphs: AmazingHiring is built for talent acquisition teams and IT recruiters who need to source technical candidates beyond standard job boards and LinkedIn.

enrichment.short_description: Payhawk is a spend management platform combining corporate cards, expenses, travel, accounts payable, and procurement with AI.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Payhawk unifies corporate cards, expenses, travel, AP, and procurement with AI automation for 6,000+ companies across Europe.

features.title: Corporate Cards
features.description: Cards with built-in spend controls.

features.title: Business Accounts
features.description: Multi-currency accounts and cross-border payments.

features.title: Expense Management
features.description: Automated receipt capture and report generation.

features.title: Accounts Payable
features.description: Invoice automation and approval routing.

features.title: Business Travel
features.description: Booking and management of corporate travel.

features.title: Procurement
features.description: Pre-spend approval workflows.

features.title: AI Workflow Orchestration
features.description: AI-driven receipt chasing, approval routing, and policy enforcement.

features.title: ERP Integrations
features.description: Connects with NetSuite, Microsoft Dynamics 365, Sage Intacct, and Workday.

pros: Modular pricing lets companies pay only for the modules they use (Travel, Cards & Expenses, Bill Pay, Procure to Pay)
Native integrations with major ERPs (NetSuite, Dynamics 365, Sage Intacct, Workday)
AI handles receipt chasing, approval routing, and policy enforcement
Used by 6,000+ companies including Vinted and MAN Truck and Bus
iOS and Android apps available
cons: Most plans require contacting sales for a custom quote
Only the Growth Program (UK/EEA small businesses, up to 20 employees) has a publicly listed price
Growth Program requires a fixed 24-month contract term

faqs.question: What does Payhawk do?
faqs.answer: Payhawk is a spend management platform that combines corporate cards, expense management, accounts payable, travel, and procurement in one system.

faqs.question: How much does Payhawk cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is modular and mostly quote-based; the Growth Program for small UK/EEA businesses is listed at £149/month with a 7-day free trial.

faqs.question: Does Payhawk use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Payhawk uses AI for receipt chasing, approval routing, and policy enforcement.

faqs.question: Where is Payhawk based?
faqs.answer: Payhawk is headquartered in London, UK, with additional offices across Europe.

faqs.question: Does Payhawk integrate with ERPs?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with NetSuite, Microsoft Dynamics 365, Sage Intacct, and Workday.

use_cases.title: Controlling multi-entity spend
use_cases.description: Finance teams at companies operating across multiple countries use Payhawk's multi-entity management and global visibility.

use_cases.title: Getting started with a fixed-price plan
use_cases.description: Small UK/EEA businesses under 20 employees use the Growth Program for a fixed monthly price.

features: Fixed 24-month term for UK/EEA small businesses

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Payhawk?
paragraphs: Payhawk is a spend management platform that brings corporate cards, expenses, business travel, accounts payable, and procurement into one system, with AI used to automate receipt chasing, approval routing, and policy enforcement.
Pricing is modular: companies choose core modules (Travel, Cards & Expenses, Bill Pay, Procure to Pay) and pay based on usage, though a fixed-price Growth Program exists for small UK/EEA businesses.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Payhawk is for
paragraphs: Payhawk serves finance teams at companies managing spend across multiple entities, currencies, and countries, from small businesses to large multi-entity organizations.

enrichment.short_description: Airwallex is a global financial platform for multi-currency accounts, international transfers, corporate cards, and spend management.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Airwallex offers multi-currency business accounts, global transfers, and corporate cards, with a free Explore plan used by 200,000+ businesses.

features.title: Global Accounts
features.description: Multi-currency accounts to receive funds in 20+ currencies.

features.title: FX & Transfers
features.description: International transfers to 200+ countries at interbank rates.

features.title: Corporate Cards
features.description: Multi-currency company and employee cards.

features.title: Bill Pay
features.description: Automated accounts payable management.

features.title: Payment Checkout
features.description: Low-code, conversion-optimized payment form.

features.title: Payment Links
features.description: No-code payment acceptance.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Global invoice generation with 160+ local payment methods.

features.title: AI Expense Policy Agent
features.description: Always-on AI agent enforcing spend policy across entities and currencies (Grow plan).

pros: Free Explore plan with no monthly fee and up to 10 Spend users
Multi-currency accounts across 20+ currencies with interbank FX rates
Free batch transfers to 120+ countries on the free plan
Used by 200,000+ businesses including Shein and Canva
AI expense policy agent available on the Grow plan
cons: Payment processing fees apply on all plans (2.80%-4.30% + $0.30 per transaction)
Advanced approval workflows and the AI expense agent require the paid Grow plan
Top-tier Accelerate plan is quote-only

faqs.question: Is Airwallex free?
faqs.answer: Airwallex's Explore plan is free at $0/user/month and includes multi-currency accounts and cards.

faqs.question: What does the Grow plan add?
faqs.answer: Grow adds advanced approval workflows, an AI expense policy agent, bill payment automation, and NetSuite/Dynamics 365 integration for $12/user/month plus a platform fee.

faqs.question: How many currencies does Airwallex support?
faqs.answer: Airwallex offers global accounts in 20+ currencies and transfers to 200+ countries.

faqs.question: Does Airwallex charge card processing fees?
faqs.answer: Yes — 2.80% + $0.30 for domestic cards and 4.30% + $0.30 for international cards, per the pricing page.

faqs.question: What languages does Airwallex support?
faqs.answer: The site is available in English, French, German, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Japanese, Spanish, Dutch, Vietnamese, and Korean.

use_cases.title: Receiving international payments
use_cases.description: Ecommerce and marketplace businesses use Global Accounts to receive customer payments in local currencies.

use_cases.title: Automating spend policy
use_cases.description: Finance teams use the AI expense policy agent to enforce rules across entities and currencies.

features: Unlimited multi-currency corporate cards, no international fees

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Airwallex?
paragraphs: Airwallex is a financial platform for global businesses that provides multi-currency accounts, international transfers, corporate cards, bill pay, and payment acceptance tools.
It offers a free Explore plan alongside paid Grow and Accelerate tiers that add automation, an AI expense policy agent, and ERP integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Airwallex is for
paragraphs: Airwallex serves ecommerce, SaaS, marketplace, travel, and fintech businesses that need to receive, hold, and pay out money across multiple currencies and countries.

enrichment.short_description: Mercury is an online banking platform for startups offering free business checking, savings, credit cards, and AI-assisted bill pay.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mercury offers free business banking with checking, savings, and AI-powered bill pay, trusted by 300,000+ startups and entrepreneurs.

features.title: Business Checking & Savings
features.description: No minimum balances or monthly fees.

features.title: Credit Cards
features.description: 1.5% cashback with no personal guarantees required.

features.title: Virtual Cards
features.description: Instant card creation.

features.title: Payment Processing
features.description: No fees on USD wires.

features.title: Invoicing & Accounting Automations
features.description: Syncs with QuickBooks, Xero, and NetSuite.

features.title: Expense Management
features.description: Team spend controls and approval workflows.

features.title: Treasury
features.description: Yield up to 3.64% via Mercury Advisory (requires $250K+ balance).

features.title: AI Bill Pay
features.description: AI reads and populates bill details and categorizes transactions.

pros: Free core banking with no monthly fees or minimum balances
4.9 rating on the Apple App Store
Used by 300,000+ entrepreneurs, processing $20B+ in monthly transaction volume
AI-assisted bill pay and transaction categorization
Integrates with QuickBooks, Xero, and NetSuite
cons: Free plan limits expense reimbursements to 5 active users/month
Unlimited invoicing API and a dedicated relationship manager require the $299/month Pro plan
Treasury yield product requires a $250K+ balance

faqs.question: Is Mercury free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mercury's core business banking plan is $0/month with no minimum balance or overdraft fees.

faqs.question: What does Mercury Plus include?
faqs.answer: Mercury Plus ($29.90/month) adds recurring invoices, an invoicing API for up to 500 invoices/month, and reimbursements for up to 20 users.

faqs.question: Does Mercury use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mercury uses AI to read and populate bill details and to categorize transactions automatically.

faqs.question: What accounting tools does Mercury integrate with?
faqs.answer: Mercury syncs with QuickBooks, Xero, and NetSuite.

faqs.question: How many businesses use Mercury?
faqs.answer: Mercury states it's trusted by 300,000+ entrepreneurs and that 1 in 3 startups choose Mercury.

use_cases.title: Free startup banking
use_cases.description: Early-stage startups use Mercury's no-fee checking and savings accounts to manage runway.

use_cases.title: Automating bill pay
use_cases.description: Finance teams use Mercury's AI bill pay to read invoices and auto-categorize transactions.

features: Free ACH, wire, and check payments
Free expense reimbursements (up to 5 active users/month)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mercury?
paragraphs: Mercury is an online banking platform built for startups and scaling companies, offering business checking, savings, credit cards, payments, and invoicing with no minimum balance requirements.
It layers AI on top of core banking to auto-populate bills and categorize transactions, and offers paid Plus and Pro tiers with additional invoicing and reimbursement capacity.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mercury is for
paragraphs: Mercury serves startups, SaaS companies, ecommerce brands, agencies, VC funds, and other scaling businesses that need free core banking with room to add invoicing and treasury features as they grow.

enrichment.short_description: Novo is a free business banking platform for small business owners with checking, invoicing, and expense tracking.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Novo offers free business banking with checking, invoicing, and AI-assisted expense tracking, trusted by 250,000+ small businesses.

features.title: Business Checking
features.description: Free checking account with no monthly fees.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Create and send invoices, accept payments.

features.title: Vendor & Employee Payments
features.description: Send payments directly from the account.

features.title: Business Cards
features.description: Debit and credit cards.

features.title: Expense Tracking
features.description: Automated expense categorization.

features.title: Novo Reserves
features.description: Set aside funds for taxes or specific goals.

features.title: Business Loans
features.description: Loan options for small businesses.

features.title: Tool Integrations
features.description: Connects accounting, payments, and payroll tools.

pros: No monthly fees for the core business checking account
4.8 rating on the App Store and 4.5 on Google Play
AI-assisted onboarding and expense categorization powered by Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini
Trusted by 250,000+ independent businesses
Novo Reserves feature for setting aside funds automatically
cons: Banking services are provided through a partner bank (Middlesex Federal Savings, F.A.) rather than Novo directly
Specific third-party integrations aren't individually named on the site
Business loan rates and terms aren't published publicly

faqs.question: Is Novo really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Novo advertises free business checking with no monthly fees for the core account.

faqs.question: Does Novo have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Novo has iOS (4.8 rating) and Android (4.5 rating) apps.

faqs.question: Does Novo use AI?
faqs.answer: Novo states it uses AI powered by Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini for onboarding and expense categorization.

faqs.question: Who holds Novo deposits?
faqs.answer: Novo banking services are provided by Middlesex Federal Savings, F.A., a Member FDIC bank.

faqs.question: How many businesses use Novo?
faqs.answer: Novo states it's trusted by 250,000+ independent businesses.

use_cases.title: Free banking for solo business owners
use_cases.description: Independent business owners use Novo's free checking account to avoid monthly banking fees.
use_cases.audience: Solo founders and independent business owners

use_cases.title: Setting aside funds automatically
use_cases.description: Business owners use Novo Reserves to automatically set aside money for taxes or specific expenses.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Novo?
paragraphs: Novo is a business banking platform offering a free checking account, invoicing, expense tracking, and business loans for small business owners.
Banking services are provided through partner bank Middlesex Federal Savings, F.A., while Novo layers on invoicing, payments, expense categorization, and AI-assisted onboarding.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Novo is for
paragraphs: Novo is built for independent business owners and small businesses looking for free checking with invoicing and expense tracking built in.

enrichment.short_description: Relay is business banking software offering up to 20 checking accounts, high-yield savings, and expense management tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Relay offers free business banking with up to 20 checking accounts, high-yield savings, and paid Grow/Scale plans for teams.

features.title: Multiple Checking Accounts
features.description: Up to 20 (50 on Scale) checking accounts with auto-transfer rules.

features.title: High-Yield Savings
features.description: Automated savings rules with APY up to 3.00%.

features.title: Visa Cards
features.description: Debit and credit cards with spend limits and real-time tracking.

features.title: Expense Management
features.description: Receipt capture and accounting software sync.

features.title: Accounts Payable
features.description: Bill uploads, delegation, and automated approvals.

features.title: Accounts Receivable/Invoicing
features.description: Flexible payment options for invoices.

pros: Free Starter plan with no monthly fee and up to 20 checking accounts
Accounting integrations with Xero, QuickBooks Online, and Gusto
14-day free trial on paid Grow and Scale plans
Serves 110,000+ businesses with $1B+ in managed deposits
Up to 50 checking accounts on the Scale plan
cons: Higher savings APY (up to 3.00%) requires the paid Scale plan
Recurring invoices and bookkeeping automations aren't available on the free Starter plan
Scale plan price shown is a limited-time discounted rate

faqs.question: Is Relay free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Relay's Starter plan is free and includes up to 20 checking accounts and 1.11% APY on savings.

faqs.question: What does the Grow plan add?
faqs.answer: Grow ($30/month) adds a higher 1.75% APY, multi-step approval rules, recurring invoices, and bookkeeping automations.

faqs.question: Does Relay offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Grow and Scale plans include a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: What accounting tools does Relay integrate with?
faqs.answer: Relay integrates with Xero, QuickBooks Online, and Gusto.

faqs.question: How many businesses use Relay?
faqs.answer: Relay states it serves 110,000+ businesses with over $1 billion in managed deposits.

use_cases.title: Organizing money across accounts
use_cases.description: Business owners use Relay's up to 20 checking accounts to separate funds for taxes, payroll, and operations.

use_cases.title: Team spend controls
use_cases.description: Teams use Relay's Visa cards with spend limits and real-time tracking to manage employee spending.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Relay Financial?
paragraphs: Relay is business banking software that gives businesses up to 20 (or 50 on the Scale plan) checking accounts, high-yield savings, Visa cards, and expense and invoicing tools.
It offers a free Starter plan alongside paid Grow and Scale tiers that add higher savings yields, approval workflows, and bookkeeping automations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Relay is for
paragraphs: Relay serves solopreneurs, startups, and small-to-medium teams that want to organize money across multiple accounts and add spend controls as they grow.

enrichment.short_description: Moss is a European spend management platform combining corporate cards, invoices, and reimbursements with AI-powered accounting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Moss offers a free plan for corporate cards, invoices, and reimbursements, with AI-powered pre-accounting used by 7,000+ companies.

features.title: Corporate Cards
features.description: Card issuance with built-in spend controls.

features.title: Invoice Processing
features.description: AI-powered invoice coding and automation.

features.title: Expense Reimbursements
features.description: Employee reimbursement workflows.

features.title: Budget Management
features.description: Purchase order and budget tracking.

features.title: Multi-Currency Payments
features.description: Support for 70+ currencies.

features.title: ERP Integrations
features.description: 40+ integrations including Xero, NetSuite, Dynamics, and DATEV.

features.title: AI Pre-Accounting Agent
features.description: Automates expense coding and invoice processing.

pros: Free plan for small teams (up to 3 users, 20 invoices/month)
AI-powered pre-accounting agent for expense coding
40+ integrations including Xero, NetSuite, DATEV, Personio, and Workday
Regulated by BaFin as an e-money institution
Used by 7,000+ companies
cons: Paid plans use custom, quote-based pricing rather than public rates
Free plan is capped at 3 users and 20 invoices per month
Primarily focused on the European, German-speaking market

faqs.question: Is Moss free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Moss offers a free plan for up to 3 users with unlimited cards and up to 20 invoices per month.

faqs.question: What does Moss's AI do?
faqs.answer: Moss uses an AI-powered pre-accounting agent to automate invoice coding and expense processing.

faqs.question: Who regulates Moss?
faqs.answer: Moss is operated by Nufin GmbH and regulated by BaFin, Germany's financial supervisory authority, as an e-money institution.

faqs.question: What integrations does Moss support?
faqs.answer: Moss offers 40+ integrations, including Xero, NetSuite, Dynamics, DATEV, Deel, Personio, and Workday.

faqs.question: How many currencies does Moss support?
faqs.answer: Moss supports payments in 70+ currencies.

use_cases.title: Getting started with free spend controls
use_cases.description: Small teams use Moss's free plan to issue cards and process a limited number of invoices.

use_cases.title: Automating invoice coding
use_cases.description: Finance teams use Moss's AI pre-accounting agent to code and process invoices automatically.

features: Up to 3 users with unlimited cards

pricing_plans.price: Custom (based on modules and transaction volume)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Moss?
paragraphs: Moss is a European spend management platform that combines corporate cards, invoice processing, and expense reimbursements, with an AI-powered pre-accounting agent to automate expense coding.
It offers a free plan for small teams and custom-priced paid plans billed by module and transaction volume.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Moss is for
paragraphs: Moss serves SMEs and mid-market companies across Europe, with particular emphasis on German-speaking markets, that need spend controls, invoice automation, and ERP integrations.

enrichment.short_description: Spendesk is a spend management platform unifying corporate cards, expenses, accounts payable, and procurement with AI automation.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Spendesk unifies corporate cards, expenses, AP, and procurement with AI automation, used by 3,000+ finance leaders across Europe.

features.title: Foundations Platform
features.description: Unlimited physical and virtual cards and users.

features.title: Receipt OCR Automation
features.description: Automated receipt collection and reading.

features.title: Expense Tracking & Reimbursement
features.description: Employee expense workflows.

features.title: Budget Monitoring
features.description: Real-time budget tracking.

features.title: Multi-Entity Workflows
features.description: Manage spend across multiple entities.

features.title: SSO/SAML & API Access
features.description: Enterprise access controls and API integration.

features.title: Spendesk AI Connect
features.description: AI assistant integration for reading documents and flagging anomalies.

pros: Unlimited cards and users included on the base Foundations plan
AI reads documents and suggests bookkeeping entries, flags duplicates and anomalies
Free CFO Connect Pro membership (normally €625) included for customers
Processes over £10 billion in spend for 3,000+ finance leaders
Integrates with Sage, Xero, NetSuite, SAP, and Datev
cons: No public pricing figures — requires a quote
No free trial or free plan advertised
Add-on modules (procurement, accounts payable, advanced workflows) are priced separately

faqs.question: How much does Spendesk cost?
faqs.answer: Spendesk doesn't publish fixed prices; it uses a fixed platform fee plus variable fees based on transaction volume, available only via a custom quote.

faqs.question: Does Spendesk use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Spendesk uses AI to read documents, suggest bookkeeping entries, detect duplicates, and flag anomalies, plus a Spendesk AI Connect integration.

faqs.question: What's included in the Foundations plan?
faqs.answer: Foundations includes unlimited physical and virtual cards, unlimited users, receipt OCR automation, expense tracking, budget monitoring, and API access.

faqs.question: What accounting tools does Spendesk integrate with?
faqs.answer: Spendesk integrates with Sage, Xero, NetSuite, SAP, and Datev.

faqs.question: Where is Spendesk based?
faqs.answer: The site describes Spendesk as "built, regulated, and profitable in Europe," without listing a specific headquarters city.

use_cases.title: Consolidating spend management
use_cases.description: Finance teams use Spendesk's Foundations plan to unify cards, expenses, and AP in one system.

use_cases.title: Managing multi-entity spend
use_cases.description: Finance teams at multi-entity companies use Spendesk's multi-entity workflows and budget controls.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Spendesk?
paragraphs: Spendesk is a spend management platform that unifies corporate cards, expense management, accounts payable, and procurement, with AI used to read documents, suggest bookkeeping entries, and flag anomalies.
Pricing combines a fixed platform fee with variable fees based on transaction volume, available only through a custom quote.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Spendesk is for
paragraphs: Spendesk serves CFOs, finance managers, accounts payable teams, and procurement leaders, particularly at companies in the 50-250 employee range, who need to consolidate spend management into one platform.
